completed_work: Added scope filtering to latest L1 summary discovery.
Added l1_scope passthrough to latest-context manifest assembly.
Added --scope support to build_context_pack.py, ask_provider.py, and compare_providers.py.
Updated tests for scoped latest-context wiring.
key_decisions: Use scope as the first stream boundary instead of introducing a new stream schema.
Keep global latest-context behavior unchanged when no scope is provided.
next_actions: Implement task-label/full-prompt separation before adding stricter admission gates.
open_questions: Should the next memory slice add task-label/full-prompt separation, admission gating, or topic-aware stream routing?
risks: Scope-based stream selection is explicit and safe, but not yet automatic; users must choose the correct scope manually.
summary_text: AI-Lab added the first operational multi-stream context mechanism. The existing scope field is now used as the stream boundary for latest L1 memory selection. By default, latest-context still selects the newest L1 globally; with --scope, build_context_pack.py, ask_provider.py, and compare_providers.py can select the newest EpisodeL1Summary inside a requested stream such as ai-lab-memory. The implementation preserves admission verdict metadata and rendered warrant metadata. Tests passed and the change was committed as ea5da57. This does not yet implement topic-aware retrieval, graph traversal, topological context packing, admission gating, or automatic stream routing.
